title: Applying for a CPH number
subtitle: The holding registration you need before any sheep, goats, pigs, cattle, or deer arrive.
excerpt: A County Parish Holding number identifies your land for livestock record-keeping and disease traceability. You can't legally keep sheep, goats, pigs, cattle, or deer in England without one. Applying takes about 10 minutes online and the number arrives within a few days.
sourceNotes: Rural Payments Agency, CPH registration guidance (gov.uk). DEFRA/APHA livestock registration and movement requirements.

glossaryTerms.definition: County Parish Holding number, a nine-digit reference identifying a piece of land for livestock-keeping records in England. Format: county (2 digits) / parish (3 digits) / holding (4 digits).
The UK government agency that administers the Rural Payments Service, which manages CPH registration, livestock movement records, and farm payments.

content.text: ties your land to the livestock-movement record system and identifies your holding during a disease outbreak. You need one before the first sheep, goat, pig, cattle, or deer sets foot on your land. Beekeeping, poultry only, and rabbits only do not legally require a CPH in England, though having one makes other permissions easier.
(RPA) issues CPH numbers in England. Scotland, Wales, and Northern Ireland have equivalent bodies. The application is online at ruralpayments.service.gov.uk and is free.
A Government Gateway account (create one if you don't have it, takes five minutes with a personal email address)
The address and postcode of the holding
The type of species you intend to keep
Whether you own or rent the land, either is fine

Straightforward applications processed online are usually allocated a CPH within two to five working days. If the application triggers a site visit (rare, usually only for large new agricultural units) allow longer. You cannot legally move sheep, goats, or pigs onto the holding without the CPH number, so apply before you plan to buy animals.

With a CPH in hand you can apply for the species-specific registrations: a flock mark for sheep and goats, a herd mark for pigs, a BVD certificate for cattle, and BCMS registration if you keep cattle. Each is a separate application. All of them ask for your CPH number.
